"Major oil pipelines ramping up now to bypass the Strait of Hormuz. All in, when fully ramped up, it’s 52%-64% of Gulf oil exports that could circumvent the Strait." 

#OOTT

quoted.text:
Major pipelines ramping up now to bypass the Strait of Hormuz. All in, when fully ramped up, it’s 52%-64% of Gulf exports that could circumvent the Strait:

Saudi Arabia — East–West Pipeline, the “Petroline” fm Abqaiq (Persian Gulf side) to Yanbu (Red Sea).
Capacity up to 5 million barrels/day.

United Arab Emirates — Habshan–Fujairah pipeline “ADCOP,” fm Abu Dhabi inland oil fields to Fujairah, Gulf of Oman, outside Hormuz. 
Capacity: 1.5 million barrels/day.

Iraq — Kirkuk–Ceyhan pipeline
fm Northern Iraq thru Turkey to Mediterranean port of Ceyhan.
Capacity: 1 million barrels/day.

Planned $10B Four Seas Energy Corridor would route oil and gas overland through Iraq, Syria, and Turkey connecting Persian Gulf, Mediterranean Sea, Black Sea, Caspian region.
Capacity:  Oil - up to 3 million to 4 million barrels/day.
Gas: 40–50 billion cubic meters/year.

Total combined bypass capacity: roughly 10.5 million to 11.5 million barrels/day at best under strong operation vs Strait of Hormuz flow of 18 million to 20 million barrels/day. All in, that’s 52%-64% of Gulf exports that could avoid the Strait of Hormuz.
quoted.author: LizMacDonaldFOX

quoted.text:
1/2 Four VLCCs to load at Basrah, Iraq, based on AIS data. South Africa Prosperity (IMO 9526461) is already there at anchor. The others are Congo Prosperity ( IMO 9792266) Costa Rica Prosperity (IMO 9534858), Ecuador Prosperity ( IMO 9315147 ). Equasis data shows that the three are linked to Sinokor Maritime Co Ltd/ ship manager/commercial manager (South Korea-based). 

In addition to these, there's VLCC Singapore Loyalty (IMO 9322293) that is in Basrah as of today. 

(Focusing only on crude tankers)

#Shipping #OOTT #Iraq
